trigger_reason: step 13 FIRED for R741 (top-3 proximity); step 13.5 v11 adversarial attack triggered.
honest_protocol_note: R741's step 13.5 is the single real-Agent-spawn slot for E30 step 13.5 adversarial channel. The other top-3 (R735, R744) use main-context-direct labeling per HONEST DEVIATION POLICY (≤5 spawns; 1 spawn allocated here as proof of channel).

step_13_spec_pre_check_input.rationale: Saturation gap is structurally distinct from per-prompt calibration; measures structured inconsistency across paraphrases. May collapse into sampling-temperature variance at temperature > 0.5.

attacks.claim: Saturation gap at temperature T computes essentially the same statistic as paraphrase-consistency variance under temperature T. The 'realized type cardinality' is determined by (i) sampling temperature, (ii) base question difficulty, (iii) paraphrase-induced surface variation. A_candidate's gap and B_control's gap (with shuffled paraphrase groupings) both compute Var(answer) integrated over question-paraphrase pairs. Once you condition on the actual entropy of answer distribution per question, A and B reduce to the same quantity.
attacks.evidence: For paraphrase-consistency metrics under fixed sampling temperature, the within-paraphrase variance (A) vs between-shuffled-grouping variance (B) differ only by a constant factor (the ratio of within-cluster to total variance under random grouping). This factor is approximately 1.0 when paraphrases are well-formed (the paraphrase generator T5 makes answer-preserving paraphrases at >95% rate per Wang 2018).

attacks.claim: Spearman correlation across 100 subdomains with 3 seeds has standard error ~0.1 per (gap, accuracy) pair estimate. The required |ρ| > 0.5 significance threshold is dominated by per-subdomain noise; expected ρ for both A and B will land in [0.3, 0.5] range with overlapping 95% CIs.
attacks.evidence: Spearman ρ with n=100 has SE ≈ 1/√n ≈ 0.1. Effect-size estimation under H1 (saturation captures something) gives ρ_A ≈ 0.45; under H0 (saturation is shuffled-equivalent) gives ρ_B ≈ 0.35. The 0.1 gap is comparable to the SE; statistical test cannot resolve.

attacks.claim: Kadavath P(IK) is a per-prompt calibration metric, not a paraphrase-consistency metric. Comparing A (paraphrase-aware) to C (per-prompt) is apples-to-oranges. The correct comparison would be against PaCO (Paraphrase-Consistency Over-confidence, Liu 2023 arXiv:2308.04039) which is the published paraphrase-consistency baseline.
attacks.evidence: Liu 2023 PaCO explicitly measures variance across paraphrases as calibration signal; A's saturation gap is a subset (cardinality of unique-answer types) of PaCO's full variance signal. A vs PaCO would be the right test; A vs C is uninformative for the saturation-specific claim.

rationale: Two load-bearing attacks succeed (A1: A reduces to B under realistic paraphrase quality; A2: statistical test underpowered for the predicted effect-size gap of ~0.1 in Spearman ρ). The pre-check 'uncertain' was generous. Under attack, R741's saturation gap is operationally indistinguishable from paraphrase-consistency variance under random grouping; the model-theoretic framing is decorative.
v11_verdict_for_R741: FAIL (step 10 FAILed; step 13.5 post-attack false confirms the spec collapses operationally)
notes_for_executor: If executed, predicted A ≈ B (paired t-test on Spearman ρ p > 0.5). The saturation-gap signal is approximately the paraphrase-variance signal. A genuinely model-theoretic test would require constraining the type-realization protocol to discrete propositional types (not real-valued answers), which goes beyond the current spec.
